text:
A squirrel named Rufus was collecting acorns. He gathered lots and lots. He stored them. He lined them up outside. He made a house out of them.

Later, a fox came. The fox liked acorns. He climbed onto Rufus's house.

Rufus shook. The fox didn't get hurt. They talked.

Fox: Do you think I could eat lots of those?

Ruffus: Why wouldn't you?

Fox: Wouldn't find them appetizing.

They made friends. That fox even stole some acorns.

Next time, Rufus took extra. He knew foxes liked surprises.

Fox: Thanks!

There you go.
